meta_title: For Founders: design with AI to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Finish SaaS Projects Faster (Advanced Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your SaaS. Use this advanced checklist to design with AI, leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your MVP faster than ever.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle stems from feature creep and architectural paralysis. To break it, treat your design as a high-fidelity artifact generated by AI. By leveraging Antigravity for UI prototyping and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you shift from manual coding to architecture management, ensuring your MVP reaches market velocity.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 90% finished projects—beautifully architected, yet tragically abandoned. You’ve been trapped in the 'Infinite Refinement' loop, where every new feature feels like a prerequisite for launch, but every line of code adds friction to your ship date. The industry calls it perfectionism; the market calls it a failure to launch.

It is time to pivot from manual labor to high-leverage orchestration. By integrating AI-driven design workflows, you stop writing boilerplate and start managing product outcomes. This is the blueprint for founders who prioritize revenue over code perfection, using modern tooling to bypass the bottlenecks that kill momentum.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Strip your SaaS to the single core value proposition that solves a burning problem. Use Antigravity to generate the visual schema that confirms this core loop works.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Product Architect. Generate a high-fidelity UI schema for a [SaaS Type] that focuses exclusively on [One Core Feature]. Omit all secondary features to maximize conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project spec file based on the Antigravity UI schema for the core MVP feature.'

steps.title: Architect for Speed
steps.description: Establish a robust directory structure that allows for modular expansion. We need a foundation that doesn't collapse under the weight of future updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, scalable folder structure for a Next.js/Supabase SaaS stack, emphasizing separation of concerns between client components and server actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure and install core dependencies based on the architecture plan.'

steps.title: Design-to-Code Pipeline
steps.description: Translate your AI-generated designs into functional React components. Do not touch CSS manually; let the AI handle the Tailwind implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the Tailwind CSS components for the authentication and dashboard layout. Ensure mobile-responsive design and accessible interactive elements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the UI components for the dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS as specified in the design schema.'

steps.title: Automate Data Integration
steps.description: Connect your frontend to your backend services. Avoid manual API boilerplate; use Claude Code to scaffold your database interactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define the database schema for [Core Data Object] and provide the Supabase client implementation for CRUD operations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the database schema and API routes for handling core user data.'

steps.title: Hard-Stop Deployment
steps.description: Force a deploy within 48 hours. If it's not in the production environment, it doesn't exist. Use AI to verify the build and push to Vercel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for production readiness, identify missing error handling, and suggest a deployment strategy for Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the project for production deployment and generate the final build configuration.'

pro_tips: Treat your AI prompt history as your technical documentation; never manually document what the AI can retrieve.
Force a 'No-UI-Change' rule during the final 12 hours of your sprint to prevent last-minute scope creep.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests only after the feature is deployed to a staging environment to ensure real-world usage coverage.
If you find yourself manually editing more than 50 lines of code, pause and re-prompt the model to handle the logic flow.
Prioritize 'Deploy-First' architecture; use feature flags to hide incomplete work rather than leaving it in a separate, messy development branch.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, implement a feedback loop with five initial users. Use your remaining AI credits to analyze their session recordings and iterate on the highest-friction points only.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint. The only variable left is your execution. Stop stalling and start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
